steps.title: Use circle equation form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Substitute center and radius
steps.content:
Here the center is $(17,14)$ and the radius is $8k$, so
$$(x-17)^2+(y-14)^2=(8k)^2$$

steps.title: Square the radius
steps.content:
Square both the $8$ and the $k$:
$$(8k)^2=64k^2$$
So the equation is
$$(x-17)^2+(y-14)^2=64k^2$$
The answer is B.
